articles.title: About this site
articles.description: Some additional details about the website

articles.title: Thuy Vu Dieu Lu
articles.contents:
I am a first year PhD student in Statistics at University of
California, Irvine. I graduated from Cal Poly Pomona with a Bachelor of
Science in Applied Mathematics/Statistics. As an undergrad, I
participated in research groups that focus on Markov chains and
operations research.
Links:

articles.title: Projects and Research
articles.description: Below are my projects and research experience.
articles.contents:
Projects
Shiny App 
Created a Shiny app that helps with learning simple linear
regression. The app allows the user to select one of the existing data
set, estimate a regression line to a set of variables and validate their
own estimations by examining the residuals.
R package 
Created a R package called neurovis to help facilitate
neuroscientific research. The package provides functions that generates
interactive raster plots for spike train data, heatmaps and lists of
sliding windows and row means for a matrix provided by the user.
Wine Quality Prediction


Analyzed the association between physicochemical properties and wine
preferences as well as predict wine quality based on those
properties.
Forcasting
Average Temperature of Cali, Columbia

Analyzed the monthly average temperature of Cali from 1857 to 2013,
and fitted a model that forecasted its average temperature from 2013 to
2015.
Integer Programming Problems

Formulated an integer programming problem to solve an assignment
problem where a number of health care workers needed distributing to
five hospitals in response to a medical crisis situation.
Linear
Programming for Optimizing Transportation Cost and Time

Formulated a linear programming problem with appropriate constraints
to determine the number of units needed distributing to different
locations such that transportation cost and time can be minimized.
Research experience
Markov Chains
Research group with Dr. Alan Krinik, Cal Poly Pomona, 2019 -
2020.
Studied different approaches in finding the n-step
transition probabilities of finite Markov chains that resembled a
generalized ballot box problem. Finite birth-death-like chains whose
transition probabilities were redefined to include generalized
catastrophe probabilities were also investigated.
Operations Research
Research group with Dr. Jillian Cannons, Cal Poly Pomona, 2019 -
2020.
Studied the paper “Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO)-Based Source
Seeking” and simulated PSO algorithms with some extensions to find an
optimal solution to a source-seeking problem where an electromagnetic
source was to be located by a group of robots.
